10: finalize_operation: move the instruments to the rest position as needed
11: pick_and_reorient_needle: pick up the suturing needle and reorient it to the desired pose; use both arms if needed
12: needle_entry_bite: align the suturing needle for entry and drive it into the tissue (occasionally silicon suturing training phantom) until the needle tip is fully inside
13: needle_drive_through: drive the suturing needle through the tissue (occasionally silicon suturing training phantom) until the needle tip emerges on the opposite side
14: needle_pull_out: pull the suturing needle out of the tissue (occasionally silicon suturing training phantom) until the entire needle body has fully exited
15: thread_pull_through: pull the suture thread through until an appropriate length remains for knot tying; use bimanual grasping as needed
16: knot_tying_throw1: tie the first knot throw for the simple interrupted stitch; typically a double throw, but use a single throw as needed
17: knot_tying_throw2: tie the second knot throw for the simple interrupted stitch; typically a single throw, but use a double throw as needed
18: knot_tying_throw3: tie the third knot throw for the simple interrupted stitch; typically a single throw, but use a double throw as needed
19: knot_retighten: retighten the knot as needed
110: knot_tying_throw4: tie the fourth knot throw for the simple interrupted stitch as needed
20: visualize_tissue: adjust the camera pose to obtain a clear view of the tissue and target region as needed
21: grasp_tissue: reach to the tissue and establish an initial grasp to begin cold-cut dissection
22: create_tension: apply tension to the tissue to prepare for cold-cut dissection; reapply or increase tension as needed before or during the subsequent cutting
23: cold_cut: perform cold-cut dissection using non-energy instruments, following the pre-defined marker trace when available
24: finalize_clean_up: finalize the cold-cut dissection and clear the cut tissue piece as needed
25: regrasp_tissue: regrasp the tissue when cold-cut dissection, restore tension as needed
26: scissor_reposition: when a large reposition is needed, retract the scissor and move it to a new cutting pose during cold-cut dissection
31: reach_to_grasp: move the gripper to the object, grasp it securely, and prepare to lift for peg tranfer
32: lift_and_transport: lift the object clear of the peg, then transport it to a position near the target peg
33: release_and_retract: release the object onto the target peg, then retract the gripper
34: reposition_to_next: reposition the gripper(s) and move the next position for peg transfer as needed
41: reach_to_approach: reach toward the tissue (or the object) and approach it to initiate tissue manipulation
42: grasp_tissue: grasp or regrasp the tissue (or the object) during tissue manipulation
43: lift_move_tissue: lift and move the tissue (or the object) towards the desired position when grasping during tissue manipulation
44: release_tissue: release the tissue (or the object) at the target area; or open the gripper for the next step during tissue manipulation
45: reposition_to_next: roposition the surgical instruments to prepare for the next step during tissue manipulation
63: start_plane_at_corner: initiate the dissection plane at the corner by opening a small entry using non-energy surgical instruments
64: advance_in_plane_cut: advance along the interface by cutting adhesions in the plane while maintaining traction
